tokens: In the rendezvous radar self-test and the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, is there's any chance you can see the (antenna) shadow?
The antenna is moving, is it not?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yes, and it looks like it's moving in shaft; I can't really tell, but I...The shadow's moving and the cross-pointers are moving.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Houston, the designate seems to work okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We suspect you might have drifted into a stop.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, I was wondering when you were going to send it up, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Been waiting for your call, Jim.
Okay, I've got a direct PAD (that is, for the short, one orbit rendezvous), a co-elliptic PAD, and a CSI PAD for you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, ready for the ascent PADs.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, here comes the direct: 171:37:22.36; 5530.4; 0032.0; minus 000.4; plus 37742; plus 01722; plus 58516; plus 56943; plus 0032.0; plus 0149.6; TPI is 172:29:39.00; the LM weight, 10936; Tig (time-of-ignition) one Rev late, 173:35:34.
Coelliptic Ascent PAD: 171:40:13.41; 5532.0, 0020.0, minus 000.5; plus 37742; plus 01722; plus 58550; plus 56943; plus 0020.0; the rest NA.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: CSI...(correcting himself) coelliptic rather: 171:40:13.41; 5532.0; 0020.0; minus 000.5; plus 37742; plus 01722; plus 58550; plus 56943; plus 0020.0.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: On the direct, Noun 37, TPI time, it should be 172:29:39.00.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) 172:35:08.00; 174:27, all zeros; 049.0; plus all zeros; 0155.1; 0267.0; plus 049.0; plus all zeros; plus 001.0.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: CSI PAD, 172:35:08.00; 174:27, all zips; plus 049.0; all zips; 0155.1; 0267.0; plus 049.0; plus all zips; plus 001.0.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Be advised, we had a little comm problem with the command module before he went over the hill.
We're going to be a little busy with him at AOS giving him some numbers and an uplink.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And we were wondering down here, did you get any food after you got back in?
And the medics would also like your
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Falcon, Houston, if you are having to dig the PRDs out, skip it for now, please.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The RCS (Reaction Control System) hot-fire (on Surface 12-10 and 12-11) looks okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we already have those, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I think I inverted them.
It's a spiral of 282 and a cursor of 184.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Oh, that's okay; we'll find it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Ed, I'm ready to copy an AGS K-factor (as per Surface 12-10) whenever you have it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm trying to get it for you now, Jim.
You beat me by about 15 or 20 seconds.
(Long Pause) Falcon, Houston; your AGS K-factor 170:00:00.80.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And be advised, I have some new
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, Ed; go with the radar angles.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And the reason for this, Dave, is that the command module is a little bit more in an elliptic orbit than we're used to, and I'll have some more words about your TPI burn after we look at it a little more.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Rog; his orbit's 64 by 54, about a 10-mile difference in perilune and apolune.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Houston, I'm going to turn the Ascent Batts On (as per Surface 12-12).
Ascent Batts going On now, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) And, Falcon, we will not uplink you at lift-off minus 35 (as had been planned on Surface 12-13).
You can press on with your checklist.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Al, did you get the TPI and lift-off time for the direct ascent before LOS?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let me give you the coelliptic PAD, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And GET (Ground Elapsed Time) of CSI, 172:35:08.00; and Noun 37, your Tig TPI, is 174:27, all zeros.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I understand on a direct ascent, it's lift-off, 171:37:22.36; TPI, 172:29:39.00; CSM weight, 35995; coelliptic lift-off, 171:40:13.41; CSI, 172:35:08.00; TPI, 174:27:00.00
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: (Pause) And, Al, let me advise you that, because of your orbit, the TPI is going to be non-nominal in angle.
About the same Delta-V; however, a different angle.
We'll have more words after insertion.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Do you want the gyro-torquing angles on that last
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Minus 4 balls 6, minus 00017, and minus 00017, and they were torqued out at 170:06.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Copy minus 4 balls 6, minus 3 balls 17, minus 3 balls 17, torqued at 170:06.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And give us Auto on the High Gain.
And, Al, we're not going to bother with your
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You're five square (meaning '5 by 5') to us on the relay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You ready to get us some warm chow?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll tell you, cold tomato soup isn't too good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Al, were you having any comm difficulties before LOS on your last pass.
We lost you for about 20 to 25 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I was busy down in the LEB (Lower Equipment Bay), but I didn't get any signal in the headset indicating that we'd lost S-band lock, and I had the Squelch off.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: I have some PIPA (Pulsed Integrating Pendulous Accelerometer) biases, when you'd like them.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I still have some PIPA biases I need to give you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We had a little (communications) net problem.
I have a PIPA bias update for you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You ready to watch the APS pressurize?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And we'll stand by for your call for tank two.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (The pressure in tank one) looks good.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Dave, you're Go for the direct rendezvous.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Go for direct on the PGNS.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Static fades) Falcon; you still with us?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I have a copy of numbers I have to read for you, Dave, when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, here's the readback on that, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And when you have your timeline book out, I'd like to change some range and range-rate numbers because of this ellipticity of the Command Module Orbit.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Do you want those PIPA biases loaded now?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What are the changes in the timeline book?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; the range and range-rate at insertion.
Range rate is 137; range, minus...(Correcting himself) Oh, sorry.
At plus five minutes: range, 117; range rate, minus 398.
And at ten minutes: range is 98; range rate minus 355.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: How do you read us now?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'd like you to change your 053 number to plus 01722.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Can you make your VHF check so we can handover the network, please.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We tried (at 171:32:47) and got no response.
We should be hot miked to the Endeavour.
We've had trouble on the VHF checks as he approached the mountains back there.
We usually don't get him until he's almost overhead because of the...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Understand VHF check is good now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're going to hand you over now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: How do you read on VOX?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, loud and clear, Dave, and you're Go for lift-off.
And I assume you've taken your explorer hats off, and put on your pilot hats.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're ready to do some flying.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Master Arm is On; I have 2 lights.
(Pause) Abort Stage; Engine Arm to Ascent.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Both guidance systems are good, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) It almost sounds like the wind whistling, doesn't it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, what a view of the rille, huh?
Boulder tracks coming down into it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Garbled) The only thing unusual I notice is the RCS Oxidizer manifold pressure oscillates every time the jets fire.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Your PGNS is showing a slight radial error, but...It's a little bit lower than nominal.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: A thousand (feet of horizontal velocity) to go.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Roger, in-plane only on the AGS.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll shut down on the PGNS.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, we got a Master Alarm on the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Stand by for a tweak or a trim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: PGNS says it's in a 40.6 by 8.9 (nautical mile orbit).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Guidance still looks good to us.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Falcon, your AGS still looks good.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I got you locked up on the VHF at 127.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Okay; we're pitching up the radar-track attitude.
We didn't get a lock on the way up.
We'll give you a call as soon as we get locked up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; I was watching for that, and I'll let you know.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Endeavour, this is Houston with a final update concerning the trusty LCRU on the lunar surface.
We turned it on yesterday, and it worked beautifully for about 13 minutes.
We w...we were panning around, zooming in and out, got a few more good pictures of the - the surrounding mountains, and suddenly we lost the TM (telemetry) downlink.
In fact, we lost everything in a very short time, about 1/60th of a second, almost as though someone had turned it off.
We tried - we waited awhile and tried to reactivate it, and did such things as send signals back to it to pan around, while we looked carefully on the passive seismometer for evidence of motion.
Apparently it was not responding to the signals.
The temperatures were completely normal right before it went off the air.
We're not exactly sure what happened.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I guess it completely went off, and just didn't get hung up somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We most likely popped a circuit breaker or something like that.
It's a little difficult to sort out.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Would you like us to go back up and check it for you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Knew you were going to ask.
transcript.speaker: Allen
